seoTitle: How to Export Your Contacts From QuickBooks
metaDescription: Learn how to export your customer and vendor contacts from QuickBooks so you can reuse the list in email marketing tools or a CRM.
intro: Whether you're setting up an email list or moving to a new CRM, getting your contacts out of QuickBooks in a usable format is a common need. This guide explains how to export your contact list step by step.

steps.heading: Open your customer or vendor list
steps.body: Go to the list of customers or vendors whose contact information you want to export.

steps.heading: Select the export option
steps.body: Use the export to Excel or CSV option available on the list screen.

steps.heading: Choose which fields to include
steps.body: If given the option, select the specific contact fields you need, such as name, email, and phone number.

steps.heading: Download and open the file
steps.body: Save the exported file and open it to confirm the contact details came through correctly.

faq.question: Can I export both customers and vendors at once?
faq.answer: Typically customers and vendors are exported separately since they're managed as different lists in QuickBooks.

faq.question: What file format does the export use?
faq.answer: Contact exports are usually available in Excel or CSV format, both of which work with most CRM and email tools.

faq.question: Will exported contacts include email addresses?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the email field was filled in for that contact in QuickBooks, it should appear in the export.

faq.question: Can I import the contacts back into QuickBooks later?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks also supports importing contact lists back in, using a similarly formatted file.
